g.title: Physical plan selection when Selinger DP search space exceeds memory limits.

ul.li: Complexity: $O(N^2)$ iterations, where $N$ is number of relations.
Memory: $O(N)$ space required for the plan pool.
Cross-Product Penalty: A $1000.0x$ cost multiplier is applied to joins without predicates.
Bus-Tub Heuristic: Left-deep trees are preferred by the cost model to minimize pipeline stalls.

div.p: Dev Note: Ensure JoinCost method caches results for (Pi, Pj) to avoid redundant calculations within the same iteration level.

g.title: Pool starts with N single-relation plan groups (SeqScan/IndexScan).

**Costing Equation:** $Cost(P_{ij}) = Cost(P_i) + Cost(P_j) + JoinCost(P_i, P_j)$
*Join Methods Considered:* 1. Hash Join (Equi-joins) 2. Sort-Merge Join (Ordered inputs) 3. Nested Loop Join (Fallback/Cross)
Select pair (Pi, Pj) that yields the lowest incremental cost increase.
Replace the two chosen plans with their optimal joined representation.
Physical plan selection when Selinger DP search space exceeds memory limits.
Pool starts with N single-relation plan groups (SeqScan/IndexScan).

**Costing Equation:** $Cost(P_{ij}) = Cost(P_i) + Cost(P_j) + JoinCost(P_i, P_j)$
*Join Methods Considered:* 1. Hash Join (Equi-joins) 2. Sort-Merge Join (Ordered inputs) 3. Nested Loop Join (Fallback/Cross)
Select pair (Pi, Pj) that yields the lowest incremental cost increase.
Replace the two chosen plans with their optimal joined representation.
